_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Identify foreign language terminology within computer programs (e.g., comments, variable names).
reference_document_element_description: Govern the enterprise reference data library, including controlled vocabulary management, change request processing, and version control.
comments: DCWF task intersects with WIDAI TF domain. TF-T-002 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: AI-S-011 (AI, cosine: 0.385), AI-T-009 (AI, cosine: 0.372).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.2/10 (raw 0.424). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. DCWF-only terms [comments, computer, foreign, identify, language, names] vs WIDAI-only terms [change, control, controlled, data, enterprise, govern] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.851 (P=0.847, R=0.855). Balanced P/R — the 11-word DCWF element and 16-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (97.3%). "Identify foreign language terminology within computer programs (e.g., comments," and "Govern the enterprise reference data library, including controlled vocabulary ma" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.385 ≈ STS 0.424 (gap 0.039). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [none] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 0.0%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. DCWF terms: [comments, computer, foreign, identify, language, names]. WIDAI terms: [change, control, controlled, data, enterprise, govern].
